---
title: "Program staff"
---

You'll log in to the **FHAB Staff app** at <https://fhab-web.onrender.com>.

## Roles

What you can see and do depends on your role.

| Role | Can do |
|---|---|
| **program_admin** | Everything, incl. account management and community-group API keys |
| **wb_staff** | Enter/edit reports, review submissions, cases, advisories, lab data |
| **field_staff** | Field verification + results entry |
| **lab_analyst** | Lab results / CEDEN data |
| **illness_workgroup** | Receives suspected-illness escalations |
| **ddw_staff** | Drinking-water focus |

Your data is **scoped to your Regional Water Board** — you generally see and manage your region's
records. (Cross-region report entry is allowed with a confirmation, for filing on behalf of
another board.)

## First login

1. Go to <https://fhab-web.onrender.com> and sign in with the credentials provided.
2. You land on the **Dashboard**: quick actions, and the reports you've recently worked on.
3. Note the **🔔 bell** (top right) — your notifications, including new submissions and illness
   alerts.

## The main things to test

### 1. Review the submission queue (*Submissions*)

- New community/app reports land here as **pending**.
- For each: choose a **region** and **Promote** (creates a tracked report) or **Reject**.
- Promoting carries over everything the reporter sent (location, bloom details, photo, and any
  suspected-illness info) and starts the report as *under investigation*.
- Try the filters (pending / promoted / rejected) and the **trusted groups** filter.

### 2. Enter a new report (*New report*)

- Mirrors the official MyWaterQuality bloom form: report type, water body, county, landmark,
  coordinates, bloom characteristics (size, textures, weather, signs), reporter contact,
  suspected illness, and photos.
- **Water body** has a type-ahead that suggests existing waterbodies — pick one to avoid
  duplicates; a near-duplicate name prompts a confirm.
- **County** is a controlled dropdown.

### 3. Open a report (*Reports → click one*)

- Edit field-verification details and set the **outcome / determination**.
- Add **field/lab results**, or **upload** a CEDEN lab CSV.
- Record a **response / advisory** (an advisory with "display on map" drives the public map).
- Staff-only: **reporter contact** and the **suspected illness/death** matrix.

### 4. Explore the map (*Map*)

- The map plots reports, colored by outcome. Use the **filters** to narrow it:
  - **Region**, **County**, **Outcome**, and **Advisory** dropdowns.
  - Date-range buttons — **15 / 30 / 60 / 120 days** (or All) — by observation date.
  - **Analytical data** modes: *Events with lab data*, or *Lab data without events* (unlinked lab
    samples shown as teal markers at their station, with a link to reconcile them).
- Filters apply server-side, so they reach the whole dataset. Click a marker for the report
  popup; **Clear all** resets everything.

### 5. Cases (*Cases*)

- Group related reports into a case (one region, one waterbody, one year); assign reports, set
  status/lead, and upload case-level lab data.

### 6. Lab data — ingest & reconcile (*Ingest Data*)

Bringing lab chemistry in and connecting it to events is the **focus of this round** and has its
own page: **[★ Ingesting lab data](lab-ingest.qmd)**. In brief, under **Ingest Data** you can:

- **Ingest lab email folders** — a Bend/partner folder (results spreadsheet + CoC/transmittal
  PDFs); the files are kept on the batch.
- **Upload CEDEN lab data** — a CEDEN WaterChemistry CSV (auto-links confident matches).
- **Lab batch reconciliation** — a staged CEDEN template you link group-by-group.
- **Lab data workboard** — assign / link / QA-review, geocode from the CoC, and tag routine
  sampling.
- **Bulk sample coordinates** — paste `station, lat, lon` rows to geocode many at once.

See the [dedicated page](lab-ingest.qmd) for step-by-step instructions and a lab-ingest test script.

### 7. Lab results browser (*Lab*)

- Browse **all** field and lab results across reports in one place. Filter by water body /
  analyte (search), analysis type, data type, region, sample-date range, and non-detects;
  sort by date, water body, analyte, or value; and **Download CSV** of the filtered set.

### 8. Notifications (*🔔*)

- Confirm you receive a notice when a new submission comes in (and an **⚠ illness alert** if one
  reports suspected illness). "Mark all read" clears the badge.

### 9. Open data (*Open data*)

- Download the published flat files (CSV or a zip), or view the **provisional JSON API**. There
  are six datasets: the four FHAB files, plus **CEDEN Chemistry Results** (analyte results in the
  statewide CEDEN structure) and a **crosswalk** that links each chemistry result to its
  watershed/GeoConnex and to the FHAB report/case.
- Confirm these contain **no** reporter contact / illness / veterinary data.

### 10. Admin only — Accounts, Groups & Analytes

- **Accounts:** create users and grant/revoke roles.
- **Groups:** register a community/partner group and mint an **API key** (shown once) so that
  group can submit attributed — and optionally "trusted" — reports.
- **Analytes:** curate the analyte vocabulary — edit an analyte's name/class/unit, **merge**
  aliases (e.g. "mcyE" → Microcystins) so results consolidate, and delete unused ones.

## A suggested 15-minute test script

1. Submit a test report from the CyanoSafe phone app ([Community track](community.qmd)) — include
   a suspected illness.
2. Log in to the Staff app; confirm the **🔔 bell** shows new + illness notifications.
3. Go to **Submissions**, **Promote** your test report to your region.
4. Open the new report; set an **outcome**, add a **field result**, and post an **advisory**.
5. Create a **Case** and assign the report to it.
6. Open the **Map**; filter by your region and the last 30 days, and find your report.
7. Open **Lab** and confirm your field result shows in the cross-report results browser.
8. Go to **Open data**; download **bloom-report.csv**; confirm your report is in it and that no
   reporter name/illness columns appear.
9. Note anything confusing along the way.
